symptoms.symptom_name: Acute behavioural escalation after an environmental change (new unfamiliar staff member)
symptoms.onset: ~4 hours ago, after the shift change

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clear temporal link between an unfamiliar-person trigger and escalation in a sensory-sensitive Fragile X patient.
A plausible social trigger lowers, but does not eliminate, the probability of a co-existing medical driver in a non-verbal patient.

symptoms.symptom_name: Self-injurious behaviour — repeated forceful wrist-striking against hard surfaces

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute behavioural crisis with self-injurious stereotypy
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Self-injurious stereotypy is a recognised crisis behaviour in Fragile X; here it is the presenting emergency because it is causing trauma.
The mechanism (repeated blunt self-trauma) is exactly what produces occult wrist injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: Panic with hyperventilation and high autonomic arousal (carer-observed fast breathing, racing heart, flushing)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Panic-level arousal fits an anxiety crisis; non-specific but consistent.
Tachycardia/tachypnoea can equally reflect pain, infection or distress — non-discriminating.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs (BP 135/85, HR 115, RR 24, SpO2 98%, Temp 36.8 C, Wt 75 kg)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute behavioural crisis with high autonomic arousal
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sinus tachycardia and tachypnoea with a normal temperature and saturation fit distress; sealed clinician measurement — the AI Doctor infers arousal from carer report, not a measured value. Afebrile lowers (not excludes) an infective driver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fragile X physical phenotype (long narrow face, prominent forehead/chin, large ears, joint hypermobility, pes planus, macroorchidism)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the known baseline phenotype; facial features may be partly video-observable but the full sign-set is clinician-assessed. Does not change the acute triage.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral wrist swelling and erythema without obvious deformity/crepitus/point tenderness on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Swelling/erythema at the site of repeated blunt trauma.
Absence of point tenderness/deformity and retained forceful use argue against a displaced fracture, but do not exclude it without imaging — clinician-only assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, normal eating/drinking, no recent illness, no observed focal pain/guarding on proxy screen

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A reassuring proxy screen lowers the probability of an infective or overt painful driver, but proxy-only reporting in a non-verbal patient cannot exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved consciousness with purposeful (non-ictal) stereotypic movements, responsive to voice

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Retained awareness, responsiveness and purposeful stereotypy are inconsistent with an ictal event.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Environmentally-triggered anxiety crisis with self-injurious stereotypy
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute behavioural crisis in Fragile X
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The trigger + escalating stereotypy + panic arousal together define the behavioural emergency and its likely proximate cause.
The negatives make a behavioural cause more likely but, being proxy-only and clinician-unexaminable, keep the disposition at urgent in-person review rather than remote reassurance.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical examination (behavioural crisis assessment + focused physical exam for a medical precipitant)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The only way to screen properly for pain/infection/constipation and to examine the wrists in a non-verbal patient — cannot be done via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wrist assessment +/- X-ray if injury suspected on examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Repeated blunt self-trauma with swelling warrants in-person examination and imaging if any clinical concern for fracture.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate remote/blind sedation or a directed benzodiazepine dose over the phone
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Chemical restraint must not be initiated remotely; it requires in-person assessment, least-restrictive/consent processes and post-sedation monitoring.
